I would love your feedback!  Did this guide help you get your first RPT prediction running? Was anything unclear, missing, or could be explained better? Drop a comment below or reach out directly, trust me, your feedback shapes future guides. Even a quick "worked for me" or "stuck at Step X" is incredibly helpful. Thank you for reading.

entry.title: SAP Fiori Tools 2607 Update: Skills, Fullstack AI Development, and Performance
entry.content:
The 2607 release of SAP Fiori tools focuses on AI-driven development, but there is also good news for the human tooling: faster startup, more Page Editor capabilities, and a few quality-of-life fixes. Here is what is new.

AI-Driven Development with the MCP Server for SAP Fiori

MCP support in SAP Business Application Studio

SAP Business Application Studio now includes an AI Chat capability that you can use for MCP-supported AI-driven SAP Fiori development directly from the browser. No installation is needed: the MCP server for SAP Fiori comes pre-installed in SAP Fiori and Fullstack Dev spaces, alongside the UI5 and CAP MCP servers.

Installing the MCP server locally

For local development, the MCP server for SAP Fiori is now available through the MCP registry on GitHub. You can also install it as a Claude plugin, together with our new skills (more on that below).

By the way, if you have the SAP Fiori tools extension pack installed in VS Code, you automatically have a version of the MCP server for SAP Fiori available that can be consumed by the GitHub Copilot extension.

Skills: the next layer on top of MCP

MCP servers provide the tools an AI agent can call, but tools alone do not tell the agent how to approach a complex, multi-step development job. That is the job of a skill. A skill bundles domain knowledge, step-by-step guidance, and best practices into a reusable prompt-based workflow that directs the AI through a specific task. Instead of leaving the model to figure out the right sequence of tool calls on its own, a skill encodes that knowledge explicitly, leading to more reliable and consistent results.

With this release, the SAP Fiori tools team publishes the first set of skills that complement the MCP server for SAP Fiori:

SkillWhat it does
sap-fiori-app-developmentGuides the creation and modification of SAP Fiori elements applications in CAP and standalone projects
sap-fiori-analytical-chartAdds an analytical chart and table hybrid to a List Report, supporting CAP and ABAP RAP (OData V4)
sap-fiori-add-visual-filterAdds chart-based visual filters to the filter bar or value help in SAP Fiori elements applications
sap-fiori-create-cliRuns and automates the @​sap-ux/create CLI for generating, converting, and managing Fiori projects
sap-fiori-eslint-pluginConfigures, migrates, or runs ESLint with the @​sap-ux/eslint-plugin-fiori-tools in SAP Fiori projects

The easiest way to get the skills is through the Claude plugin, where they are included alongside the MCP server for SAP Fiori. Alternatively, you can install individual skills via skills.sh, an open ecosystem for reusable agent skills that supports Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and others.

We will continuously expand and improve this set of skills, and we are very much looking forward to your feedback.

Fullstack development in VS Code

The MCP server for SAP Fiori pairs with the new ABAP MCP server, which is part of the ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code. Together, the two bridge the gap between backend ABAP services and the SAP Fiori frontend, enabling a true fullstack AI-driven development experience from within VS Code. For a concrete walkthrough of how this can look in practice, I recommend watching my ABAPConf presentation.

A new top-level tool structure

Based on feedback we have collected from usage, we restructured the top-level tools of the MCP server for SAP Fiori. More capabilities are now surfaced directly at the top level, in particular the app generators and metadata retrieval, which are needed to properly connect backend services to the frontend layer. This restructuring is available as of version 1.8.1 of the MCP server for SAP Fiori.

The top-level tools now are:

ToolWhat it does
search_docsSearches SAP Fiori elements, annotations, UI5, OPA5, and SAP Fiori tools documentation
list_fiori_appsScans a directory to find existing SAP Fiori applications
list_sap_systemsLists SAP systems stored in the user's environment
download_odata_service_metadataDownloads the EDMX metadata of an OData service from an SAP system
generate_fiori_app_odataGenerates a new SAP Fiori application for OData (non-CAP) projects, for example RAP-based services
generate_fiori_app_capGenerates a new SAP Fiori application inside an existing CAP project
list_functionality (Step 1 of 3)Lists supported modification operations for an existing SAP Fiori application
get_functionality_details (Step 2 of 3)Returns the required parameters for a chosen modification
execute_functionality (Step 3 of 3)Executes the modification with the provided parameters

This structure also sets the stage for what comes next. We are working on adaptation project support for the MCP server for SAP Fiori, which will add tools for creating and modifying adaptation projects directly through AI agents. They will join the top-level structure shown above.

Renovations and Performance Improvements

Moving from CommonJS to ECMAScript Modules

ECMAScript Modules (ESM) are the official JavaScript module standard, supported natively in modern browsers and Node.js. With this release, the SAP Fiori tools suite and its Node.js modules migrate from CommonJS to ESM. For us, this means better ecosystem compatibility, tree-shaking for smaller bundles (unused code eliminated at build time), and more efficient asynchronous module loading.

ESLint 10 upgrade

The 2603 release delivered ESLint 9 support for the tools and generator. With 2607 we take the next step and upgrade to ESLint 10, keeping the toolchain current and continuing the move toward the modern flat config format.

80% faster extension startup

As the SAP Fiori tools extensions have grown over time, startup time had crept up correspondingly. Through targeted refactoring and more efficient bundling, loading only what is necessary at startup, we reduced the startup time of the SAP Fiori tools extensions by around 80%.

Other Improvements

A few smaller additions that are worth calling out:

  • Fiori Generator error details: Clicking on an error in the generator now opens the full error details in the console, making it much easier to diagnose what went wrong.
  • Page Editor building blocks: The Page Editor now supports Page, Form, and Field building blocks in custom pages and custom sections, rounding out the building block coverage for custom development.
  • SAP Fiori elements enhancements via Page Editor: The Page Editor now lets you configure a notes reuse component, inline edit, mass edit, and creation dialogs (and more) for SAP Fiori elements applications, all through the graphical configuration interface without touching annotations manually.
  • Reuse existing LineItem annotations for tables: When adding a table, you can now select an existing LineItem annotation from the service instead of always having to create a new one. This avoids duplicate annotations and makes it easier to build UIs on top of well-annotated services.
  • Unique control IDs in XML generation: The Page Editor now correctly generates unique control IDs when producing XML for custom elements.
  • Download UI5 ABAP repository content from the public cloud: You can now download source artifacts directly from a deployed SAPUI5 ABAP repository, so you can continue local development even when the source files are not available. This closes a gap for public cloud customers, who previously had no way to import from the ABAP backend. See the SAP Help Portal documentation for more information.

Staying Up to Date

If you want to track changes in SAP Fiori tools between release blog posts, the What's New section in the SAP Help Portal is the best place to look. It is updated continuously with newly delivered capabilities and fixes.

For documentation and sample code covering all building blocks available when creating SAP Fiori elements applications, visit the SAP Fiori development portal.

Questions and feedback? Leave a comment below or reach out through the community.

entry.title: AI-Assisted Journal Upload
entry.content:
I am Betty Guedez, Product & Engineering Solution Architect at SAP Regional Implementation Group RIG. I am coming back to this community with an SAP strategic topic: Business AI.

 

            More specifically, Autonomous Finance. AI is the cornerstone of SAP Autonomous Enterprise offering, that revolutionize the way we support our customers businesses: Innovations that develop state-of the-art tools and agents to automate tasks, where humans can be released of cumbersome tasks to focus on business intelligence and financial engineering to maximize benefits with real-time value.

           

            As part of the SAP Autonomous Enterprise, SAP Autonomous Finance is built out of Joule Assistants that orchestrate all the Finance agents that support this business process. AI-assisted Journal Upload is part of Financial Closing Assistant, that drives the full close lifecycle across posting, accruals, journal validation, reconciliations, and so on. The major goal of Financial Closing Assistant is to reducing closing time and streamlines the reporting associated to closing.

 

 

Figure 1: SAP Finance Assistants-Financial Closing Assistant

 

            For years, SAP has developed solutions that enable customers to perform specific business tasks which, while executed outside the ERP system and often requiring significant time and effort, provide critical input for gaining a complete view of the business. Tasks that, if not performed, can disrupt business operations and compromise data visibility. Uploading journal entries is one of them. It is either a periodic or frequent activity or both, in every landscape: satellite systems, legacies, integrations that support the operation in a way or another, that feedback the ERP with transactions that must be posted to keep the accounting books updated.

 

            Many of us have used the different ways to upload journal entries to the ERP: The traditional file (we used to call it "flat") with a fixed structure of columns with specific format. Another is the journal entry template, for postings based on your needs, where fields and values can be selected to produce a tailored-specific template for postings, taking out non-relevant fields and focusing only on the desired ones. This template can also be shared and with pre-defined level of use (group, company, user). Please look at Fiori app Manage Journal Entry Templates (F3803)documentation to know of this, in case you are interested. Both have been extremely useful tools, widely used by customers and partners.

            Month-end journal entries upload is more needed than ever. Increasingly complex system landscapes, new integration points, constantly evolving business models, and the ever-changing business environment demonstrate that, if this process can be executed autonomously with minimal supervision, the resulting efficiency gains and cost savings can make a significant contribution to the organization's value creation efforts. 

What is AI-Assisted Journal Upload?

 

            As period-end journal entries are necessary for period closing, accounting teams are spending considerable time in curating data as per the policies and reporting needs. Through new SAP Fiori apps with Generative AI capabilities embedded, AI-assisted Journal Upload in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition accelerates the creation and the upload of period-end manual journal entries.

            AI-assisted Journal Upload is a Financial AI feature to speed-up the creation and the upload of period-end manual journal entries.

            With AI-assisted Journal Upload, accountants can:

  • Assign a guidance document (written in natural language) for posting to the journal upload case
  • Create journal upload cases
  • Generate journal posting proposals according to the assigned guidance
  • Validate the posting proposals before posting and
  • Post the posting proposal as journals

 

What are typical business cases for AI-Assisted Journal Upload?

            Business scenarios best suited for AI-assisted Journal Upload include recurring general ledger journal entries with a high volume of records and similar information requirements, making them ideal candidates for this autonomous feature. Other excellent use cases include large volumes of prepaid expenses, lease or rental in advance-payments, and customer prepayments, all of which can be efficiently processed using AI-assisted Journal Upload.

Is AI-assisted Journal Upload intended for large companies only?

 

            Not at all. Regardless of their size, Companies often pay for services they will receive in the future. Small and large businesses work with prepaid expenses frequently. It is a way to secure better terms, benefit from early-payment discounts, and take advantage of periods of stronger cash flow while ensuring futre obligations are covered.

 

            When the volume of prepaid operations is high, as they happen in advance, a proper tracking is crucial for financial control and compliance, to record them when is right to do it (in the posting period when each service is received). Plus, a posting template easy to use and populate to post to the right accounting objects, is invaluable for saving time and reducing the risk of errors during the financial closing. The easiest, the better. These are some of the advantages of using AI-assisted Journal Upload.

 

            In the graphic below, we find some interesting figures about uploading journal entries for a business (complexity varies in each case). It can be found on SAP Discovery Center - S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - AI-Assisted Journal Upload:

 

 

Figure 2: AI-assisted Journal Upload - Estimated benefits

            Technically speaking, AI-Assisted Journal Upload is an embedded Intelligent Scenario delivered via SAP's Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) framework, using scenario FIN_INTLGNT_JRNLUPLD. It is natively embedded within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, with the underlying AI service provisioned through SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) as a backend infrastructure integration — not a side-by-side deployment.

What are the specifications of AI-Assisted Journal Upload?

  • Identifier: J247
  • AI Type: AI Feature
  • Support Component: FI-GL-AI-IJU
  • Minimun Required System Version: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS03
  • Required Roles & Authorizations:
    • BTP: Global Account Administrator role, assigned via Security > Role Collections in the BTP Cockpit
    • S/4HANA: ISLM Administrator role to manage intelligent scenario activation
  • BTP Subaccount & Entitlement Requirements:
    • A valid BTP subaccount must exist and be accessible
    • The AI service entitlement must be available and assigned to the relevant subaccount prior to service instance creation

 

What do we need to start using AI-assisted Journal Upload?

 

 

            3 easy steps to determine AI-assisted Journal Upload readiness:

  • Check 1 - To verify the Scenario Status in Intelligent Scenario Management (F4470)  Fiori app, you need to confirm that FIN_INTLGNT_JRNLUPLD shows a Published status with no errors in the ISLM app
 
 

 

 

Figure 3: Intelligent Scenario Management

 
 

 

Figure 4: Manage Journal Posting Policies
Check 2 - Confirm that Fiori App Manage Journal Posting Policies (F8539) is available in Fiori Launchpad (FLP). Check role authorizations and catalogs if it is not visible

 

 
 

 

Figure 5: Manage Journal Upload Cases
Check 3 - Confirm that Fiori App Manage Journal Upload Cases (F8538) is available in Fiori Launchpad (FLP). Same considerations as F8539 here.

 

            Also, it is always good to make sure that content in SAP Note 3573144 AI-assisted Journal Upload in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private has been implemented in the system.

            Let me share the link to my blog post AI-assisted Journal Upload - How to use it 

            I hope you find this insightful. In my next blog, I will go step by step on how to use AI-assisted Journal Upload.

Thank you,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team

entry.title: AI-Assisted Journal Upload - How to use it
entry.content:
I am Betty Guedez, Product & Engineering Solution Architect at SAP Regional Implementation Group RIG. I am coming back with the second part of my blog post AI-assisted Journal Upload . This time, how to use it.

 

            Remember that AI-Assisted Journal Upload is an AI feature, part of the SAP Autonomous Finance offering.

 

            We also know that SAP Autonomous Finance is one of the Joule Assistants in SAP Autonomous Enterprise platform, and it is there to that orchestrate all the Finance agents that support this business process. AI-assisted Journal Upload is part of Financial Closing Assistant, that drives the full close lifecycle across posting, accruals, journal validation, reconciliations, and so on.

 

           

Start using AI-assisted Journal Upload

 

            AI-assisted Journal Upload requires a couple of IMG-customizing-related activities in the backend, related to the category and subcategory of the journal entries you will post. Following the path Financial Accounting => Financial Accounting Global Settings => Document => Intelligent Journal Uploads, you will see the two activities, Define Journal Upload Case Categories and Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories. Together, they both serve to give a classification to the expenses that better explain your postings.

Define Journal Upload Case Categories:

Figure 1: Define Journal Upload Case Categories

 Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories:

Figure 2: Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories


            Next, to download and adapt the policy template, we access the Fiori App Manage Journal Posting Policies (F8539). With this app, we set up all the relevant fields and the natural language instructions to populate the fields on the template.

 
 

 

Figure 3: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Initial Screen


            Click on Download Template, select the format (Spreadsheet, PDF) and OK. (I feel more comfortable with the excel file, so I chose the first one).

 
 

 

Figure 4: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Template Downloaded

            Follow the guidelines provided and update the Input instructions that the feature  is going to use to handle the data in the input file. It is important to bear in mind:

  • For now, Cardinality - Optional/Mandatory (O/M)- field is not required. It will be supported in future releases, so you may leave it blank.
  • The file name format must be "Name", followed by a "_", followed by the "Language Code".ext (xslx, pdf). Supported language codes are EN (English), DE (German), ES (Spanish), FR (French), PT (Portuguese), JA (Japanese), ZH (Simplified Chinese). Example: "Policy Document 2026_EN.xlsx".
  • Make sure that the fields referenced in the Input Instruction of a field in the policy document, are columns in the input data file, for example:
 
 

 

Figure 5: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Template - Input Instruction examples

            Based on the instruction for field WRBTR, there should be a column labeled "Amount" with values in the input file.


            And now, let's work with the policy in the system:

Figure 6: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Policy Creation

Case category and Subcategory: According to customizing mentioned above

Description: Short Text about the policy purpose (max 50 characters)

Valid to: Defaulted to 12/31/9999

Valid from: Starting validity date of the policy. If there is an existing policy of the same Category and Subcategory, with Valid from date prior or equal to this, the system will raise an error, and the policy cannot be saved. The policy must start after the starting date of any previously created policy of this service.

 
 

 

Figure 7: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Policy Activation

 

 

            Once created, the policy needs to be Activated.

            Now, it is the time to upload the input data to post the journal entries.

            We designed a simplified excel file that contains the data of the charges received for shuttle services for 3 employees, to create the postings for the expenses charged for this service:

Figure 8: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Sample input data


            To do that, open the Fiori app Manage Journal Upload Cases (F8538) and hit Create:

Figure 9: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Updating the Posting Proposal

            Once created, a Proposal must be generated:

Figure 10: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Generating the Posting Proposal

            Proposal Validation:

Figure 11: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Validating the Posting Proposal

            And proposal finally to be posted:

Figure 12: Manage Upload Cases: Posting the Proposal

           

            Let's display the postings in the Fiori app Manage Journal Entries (F0717A):

 

Figure 13: Manage Journal Entries: Displaying the Postings

 

            The posted document SA 1000000006 shows that rides of the three employees using the shuttle bus service in the second week of June 2026 were posted to the right posting period (06/2026), crediting the prepaid expense (which is currently a right, in consequence) for $264,00 USD.

            AI-assisted Journal Upload is a key component of the SAP Autonomous Enterprise offering and provides an easy, straightforward process to generate postings in S/4HANA from external sources, as part of the month-end closing, releasing the business experts from spending lots of time and effort fulfilling templates that can be well known, but still somewhat error-prone.

            Before closing, let me share two more useful resources with you.

SAP AI Services in Discovery Center

AI-assisted Journal Upload in help.sap.com

            I hope you find this insightful.

Thank you,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team

entry.title: Get Ahead of What's Next: Join the Early Release Series
entry.content:
Be First to Know What’s Next

Curious about the innovations shaping the future of supply chain? The Early Release Series gives you a front row seat. It's a biannual event where SAP product experts walk you through what's coming next in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, before everyone else.

What You'll Get Out of It

Across several days, you can join expert-led demos, gain insider insights, and take part in real-time Q&A. That means early access to new features and direct answers from the people building them. It's a practical way to see live demos, ask your questions, and plan ahead with confidence.

Sessions for the SCM Community

Here are four sessions we think you'll find valuable. Each one is a detailed, expert-led deep dive.

Artificial Intelligence — July 13, 2026, 12:30pm – 2:00pm CET

Take a broader look at where AI is heading. This session covers the Autonomous Enterprise, Joule for Business, workflow automation, and Autonomous SCM, including production, planning, and inventory agents that connect directly to your supply chain work.

Supply Chain Management — July 16, 2:00pm – 2:45pm CET

Discover the latest innovations in Supply Chain Management and see how they can support your day-to-day operations.

Manufacturing — July 14, 4:00pm – 4:45pm CET

Explore the newest manufacturing innovations, from engineering and planning to shop-floor execution. You'll see how AI-driven manufacturing agents help streamline decisions, automate routine tasks, and strengthen operational resilience. Expect practical demos, insider tips, and a clear view of what's next, so you can accelerate value from day one.

Quality Management — July 16, 4:30pm – 5:15pm CET

Get up to speed on the latest innovations in Quality Management and what they mean for your processes.

 

How to Sign Up

An active SAP Learning Hub license is required to register for the live sessions and to watch the recordings afterward.

If you're a customer:

You'll need access to at least the SAP Enterprise Support edition. Here's how to sign up:

  1. Go to the sign-up page and select Sign up.
  2. Authenticate with your S-user ID and password.
  3. The system checks your eligibility, creates your SAP Learning Hub user, and populates your learning catalogue. This step can take up to 2 hours.
  4. You'll get a confirmation e-mail once registration is complete.

If you're a partner:

With a valid SAP Universal ID and access to the Empowering SAP Partners site, you can join the live sessions and recordings directly.

Don't Miss Out

These sessions won't wait, and neither should you. Secure your spot to gain early insights, watch live demos, and stay ahead with the newest SAP updates.

Register today and be first to know what's next: learning.sap.com

entry.title: SAP Fiori Development newsletter issue #41 (July 2026)
entry.content:
Summer is here and it’s getting hot. I’m not just talking about the weather. AI is heating up both for the companies that create the large language models and everyone trying to keep up with the latest innovations. All while managing their token costs. SAP is embracing AI for our internal application development and the tools we provide customers and partners for building their own applications.

Sapphire produced several major announcements around our development tools, the biggest being Joule Studio 2.0. Joule Studio 2.0 is SAP’s AI-first development environment for building custom AI agents, apps, and workflows. Powered by the SAP Business AI Platform.

As the development landscape shifts, we want to know what’s important to you. We are interested in how well SAPUI5 meets your needs today, which UI experiences you expect to dominate enterprise apps in the coming years, and which capabilities matter most for your future AI-powered development work. Please take this 5-minute survey.

If you want to join other developers to discuss various traditional and AI-powered Fiori development-related topics from the comfort of your office or home, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable. Register online or email me for an invitation.

Development News

Optimizing SAP Fiori Elements for Mobile Phone Users

While SAP Fiori elements applications are responsive, they adjust controls based on screen size and device type, it does not provide an optimized experience for users. We recently reimagined the SAP Fiori elements mobile experience to include native mobile feel, intuitive navigation, and smarter mobile-optimized layouts. Read more.

Develop RAP-Based SAP Fiori Applications in Minutes with SAP Fiori Tools AI Project Accelerator

Developing enterprise SAP Fiori applications using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) provides a standardized way to build modern, cloud-ready applications. These applications are typically developed in environments such as SAP BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (version 2602 or higher). The Project Accelerator now supports RAP-based projects. It is available in SAP Business Application Studio under an SAP Build Code subscription. Learn more.

TypeScript 6 and 7 – What UI5 TypeScript Developers Need to Know in 2026

TypeScript is going through its biggest transition in years — and if you build UI5 applications with TypeScript, here we have all the details you need to know. Read about it in this blog post.

UI5 Plugins for Coding Agents: Now Supporting GitHub Copilot

The UI5 Plugins for Coding Agents — previously known as UI5 Plugins for Claude — are now also available for GitHub Copilot. The plugins bring up-to-date SAPUI5 and OpenUI5 knowledge directly into your AI-assisted development workflow, whether you work with Claude Code or GitHub Copilot. Version 0.1.3 also introduces three new skills covering UI Integration Cards, OPA5 integration tests, and OpenUI5 tables. Read more.

Introducing the UI5 Modernization Plugin

Keeping your SAPUI5 or OpenUI5 app up to date just got easier. The new UI5 Modernization Plugin automates the most time-consuming parts of app modernization: replacing deprecated APIs, eliminating global namespaces, updating manifest.json and Component.js, and getting your app ready for Content Security Policy compliance. The plugin is available as a Claude Code plugin and in the GitHub Copilot marketplace. Read the full walkthrough.

 What's next for UI technology in the age of AI?

We're running a short survey to collect input from developers, architects, and UI technology enthusiasts in the SAP ecosystem — and we want to hear from you. Topics include how well SAPUI5 meets your needs today, how AI tools work with your current UI stack, and which capabilities matter most for your future development work. Shape the SAPUI5 road map by answering few questions in just 5 minutes, open July 1–19. Take the survey.

Joule Studio 2.0 announced at Sapphire

SAP announced its latest development environment for building apps, agents, and workflows. Read how it changes how you build apps.

SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities including agentic capabilities is now available!

ABAP AI in SAP Joule for Developers include agentic capabilities and is now offered as a service across SAP BTP ABAP Environment, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for releases 2021 and higher. Joule speaks ABAP, and now it speaks more than ever before. See the details.

Upgrading your SAPUI5 Rich Text Editor: Beyond TinyMCE version 4

The Rich Text Editor is an SAPUI5 control for data input and editing. It is used to enter rich text with different styles, colors, and formatting. The Rich Text Editor allows users to format text and add different types of elements, such as images and hyperlinks. he SAPUI5 team further plans to permanently remove TinyMCE version 4 from the SAPUI5 code later in 2026. Learn how to adjust your code to avoid any problems.

Events

The future of UX is here. Are you ready for an AI -powered world?

At Sapphire 2026 I had the opportunity to speak with many customers and partners about their reaction to the way we are moving with our UX strategy. Generally, people are excited about the direction we are going and having more automation to free up time for deeper thought and analysis, but they are concerned about trusting AI to do things that might have significant business impact. Read my full analysis.

SAP Connect Day for UX in Silicon Valley focused on AI strategy and tactics for implementation

SAP Connect Day for UX in Silicon Valley connected customers with SAP experts and selected partners to discuss how AI will improve the lives of SAP Cloud ERP users. Read about highlights and where you can learn more about the topics we discussed.

Back issues from the past year

SAP Fiori Development Newsletter issue #40 (May 2026)

SAP Fiori Development newsletter March 2026 (issue #39)

SAP Fiori development newsletter January 2026 (issue #38)

SAP Fiori development newsletter November 2025 (issue #37)

SAP Fiori development newsletter September 2025 (issue #36)

SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2025 (issue #35)

entry.title: Context Engineering in SAP Analytics Cloud: Building ContextWallet for AI and Genie Integrations
entry.content:
Introduction

 

 

 

Over the last few years, the conversation around analytics has shifted dramatically. Traditional dashboards are no longer viewed as the final destination for business intelligence. Instead, organizations are increasingly exploring how Artificial Intelligence can work alongside analytics platforms to provide conversational insights, automated explanations, and faster decision-making.

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is already a powerful platform for planning, reporting, and analytics. At the same time, AI-powered solutions such as Databricks Genie and Large Language Models (LLMs) are enabling users to interact with data using natural language rather than traditional reports and dashboards.

However, while experimenting with SAC and AI integrations, I encountered a challenge that is rarely discussed but becomes immediately apparent once you start building enterprise AI solutions.

The challenge is not generating answers.

The challenge is providing the AI with the right business context.

Imagine a finance analyst working inside a SAC dashboard. Before analyzing any KPI, the analyst typically applies several business filters such as:

  • Company Code

  • Fiscal Year

  • Version

  • Currency

  • Profit Center

  • Account Type

These selections define the meaning of every chart, table, and KPI displayed on the dashboard. In many cases, the same metric can produce completely different interpretations depending on the selected filters.

Now consider a scenario where the analyst asks an AI assistant:

Why is EBITDA decreasing?

For a human looking at the dashboard, the question is obvious because the context is already visible on the screen. The user knows which company, fiscal year, and reporting version are currently selected.

For the AI, however, the question is incomplete.

Without access to the dashboard context, the AI does not know:

  • Which company is being analyzed

  • Which fiscal year is selected

  • Which reporting version is active

  • Which currency is being used

  • Which business entity is currently in focus

As a result, organizations often face a disconnect between analytics systems and AI systems. The dashboard contains the business context, while the AI receives only the question.

This observation led me to think about a concept that I call Context Engineering.

Just as Prompt Engineering focuses on designing effective instructions for AI systems, Context Engineering focuses on ensuring that AI systems receive the right business context before generating a response.

In enterprise analytics, context is often more important than the question itself. A perfectly crafted prompt can still produce an inaccurate answer if the AI lacks awareness of the user's current analytical context.

To explore this idea further, I started building a custom SAP Analytics Cloud widget called ContextWallet.

The goal of ContextWallet is simple:

Capture, preserve, and share dashboard context across SAC applications, pages, and AI systems.

Instead of repeatedly passing filters between dashboards, scripts, and external AI services, ContextWallet acts as a centralized context layer that can store business selections, maintain state across pages, create reusable snapshots, and prepare structured context payloads for AI assistants such as Databricks Genie.

While ContextWallet began as an experiment, it quickly evolved into something more interesting: a practical implementation of Context Engineering within SAP Analytics Cloud.

In the following sections, I will discuss the motivation behind this approach, the architecture of ContextWallet, and how it can help bridge the gap between traditional analytics and AI-powered decision-making.

The Missing Piece: Why Prompt Engineering Alone Is Not Enough

 

 

 

When discussing Generative AI, one concept appears almost everywhere: Prompt Engineering.

Prompt Engineering focuses on designing better instructions for AI systems in order to obtain more accurate, relevant, and useful responses. As Large Language Models became more capable, organizations quickly realized that the quality of the prompt significantly influences the quality of the response.

For example, instead of asking:

Explain revenue performance.

A more effective prompt might be:

Explain the revenue performance for Company Code 1000 in Fiscal Year 2025 and identify the top three contributing factors.

The second prompt provides more information and therefore allows the AI to generate a more focused response.

However, while experimenting with AI-driven analytics scenarios, I realized that prompt quality is only one part of the problem.

Even the most carefully designed prompt can fail if the AI lacks awareness of the user's current business context.

Consider a typical SAP Analytics Cloud dashboard used by a finance team.

A user may already have selected:

  • Company Code = 1000

  • Fiscal Year = 2025

  • Version = Actual

  • Currency = USD

  • Entity = EU_Corp

The dashboard understands these selections perfectly. Every chart, table, KPI, and calculation is already operating within this context.

Now the user asks:

Why is revenue decreasing?

From a human perspective, the question is completely valid because the context is already visible on the screen.

From the AI's perspective, however, the question is ambiguous.

Revenue for which company?

Which fiscal year?

Which version?

Which entity?

Without access to the dashboard context, the AI may either produce a generic answer or require the user to repeat information that already exists within the application.

This creates an inefficient user experience.

The user already provided the context through filters and selections, yet the AI remains unaware of it.

This is where I believe a new discipline becomes necessary.

Introducing Context Engineering

If Prompt Engineering is about optimizing the question, Context Engineering is about optimizing the information surrounding the question.

Context Engineering focuses on ensuring that AI systems receive the right business context before generating a response.

In analytics applications, context may include:

  • Active dashboard filters

  • User selections

  • Input control values

  • Reporting periods

  • Organizational hierarchies

  • Planning versions

  • Saved dashboard states

  • Business entities

Rather than repeatedly asking users to provide this information, the application should be capable of capturing and managing it automatically.

In other words, before asking:

Why is EBITDA decreasing?

the system should already know:

Company Code: 1000
Fiscal Year: 2025
Version: Actual
Currency: USD
Profit Center: PC001

This transforms AI interactions from generic conversations into context-aware business discussions.

The Role of Context in Enterprise AI

Unlike public AI use cases, enterprise AI systems rarely operate in isolation.

They exist within business applications, planning systems, reporting platforms, and analytical environments.

As a result, context often becomes more valuable than the prompt itself.

A simple question with rich business context can generate a highly relevant response.

Conversely, a sophisticated prompt with no context may generate a vague or inaccurate answer.

This realization became the foundation for ContextWallet.

Rather than treating context as something temporary and scattered across filters, pages, and widgets, I wanted to create a centralized context layer that could capture, preserve, and share business context across SAP Analytics Cloud applications and future AI integrations.

The objective was not simply to build another utility widget.

The objective was to experiment with Context Engineering as a practical architectural pattern for AI-powered analytics.

Introducing ContextWallet

ContextWallet

 

After identifying the need for a dedicated context layer within SAP Analytics Cloud, I began exploring possible implementation approaches.

The primary objective was not to create another utility widget or temporary storage mechanism. Instead, I wanted to build a reusable framework that could act as a centralized repository for business context and eventually serve as a bridge between SAC dashboards and AI-powered analytics systems.

This idea became ContextWallet.

ContextWallet is a custom SAP Analytics Cloud widget designed to capture, preserve, manage, and share business context across dashboard pages, applications, and external AI integrations.

At its core, ContextWallet acts as a lightweight context management engine that stores the current analytical state of an application in a structured format.

For example, a finance dashboard may contain the following business selections:

{
  "CompanyCode": "1000",
  "FiscalYear": "2025",
  "Version": "Actual",
  "Currency": "USD",
  "Entity": "EU_Corp"
}

Rather than keeping these values scattered across input controls, filters, dropdowns, and application variables, ContextWallet consolidates them into a single centralized context object.

This context can then be accessed by any component within the application and can be passed directly to AI systems whenever required.

Core Design Principles

While designing ContextWallet, I focused on four fundamental principles.

1. Centralized Context Management

Modern analytical applications often contain dozens of user selections spread across multiple pages.

Without a centralized mechanism, each component must independently manage and retrieve these values.

ContextWallet provides a single source of truth for business context.

Instead of querying multiple controls throughout the application, developers can retrieve the entire context through a single interface.

2. Cross-Page Persistence

One of the challenges in multi-page SAC applications is maintaining context while navigating between pages.

A user may define a specific analytical scenario on one page and expect it to remain available throughout the application.

ContextWallet addresses this by persisting context within the browser session, allowing information to remain accessible across multiple dashboard pages.

This creates a more seamless user experience while reducing the need for repetitive filter selections.

3. Snapshot-Based State Management

Analytical workflows frequently involve recurring review scenarios.

Examples include:

  • Month-End Reviews

  • Quarterly Business Reviews

  • Forecast Reviews

  • Budget Planning Sessions

To support these use cases, ContextWallet introduces a snapshot mechanism.

Snapshots allow users or developers to capture the complete dashboard context at a specific point in time and restore it later.

This capability enables reproducible analytical scenarios and creates the foundation for future context comparison capabilities.

4. AI-Ready Context Generation

Perhaps the most important design goal was preparing SAC applications for AI integrations.

Traditional dashboards and AI systems often operate independently, even though they are working with the same business data.

ContextWallet helps bridge this gap by transforming dashboard selections into structured context payloads that can be consumed by AI assistants.

For example, when a user asks:

Why is revenue decreasing?

ContextWallet can automatically attach the active business context before sending the request to an AI system.

Instead of receiving only a question, the AI receives both the question and the analytical context in which the question was asked.

This significantly improves the relevance and accuracy of generated responses.

5. Future AI Security and Session Management

As organizations move toward AI-powered analytics, context management will become increasingly important not only for usability but also for security and governance.

Today, ContextWallet primarily focuses on capturing and managing dashboard context. However, its architecture opens opportunities for future enhancements related to AI session management and role-based AI interactions.

In enterprise environments, different users often require access to different AI capabilities depending on their responsibilities.

For example:

  • Finance Analysts may interact with financial planning models.

  • Controllers may access variance analysis assistants.

  • FP&A teams may work with forecasting and budgeting copilots.

  • Executives may receive high-level strategic insights.

  • Operations teams may interact with supply chain or operational AI models.

Rather than exposing a single AI model to every user, future implementations could leverage ContextWallet as a session-aware context layer capable of managing AI routing and authorization.

A session identifier or secure context token could be maintained alongside business context, allowing applications to determine:

  • Which AI model should receive a request.

  • Which data domains are accessible.

  • Which analytical capabilities are available.

  • Which business entities a user is authorized to view.

In this scenario, ContextWallet would not simply store dashboard filters but would become part of a broader AI governance framework.

A simplified future architecture could look like this:

User
  │
  ▼
SAP Analytics Cloud
  │
  ▼
ContextWallet
  │
  ├── Business Context
  ├── Session Context
  ├── Security Context
  └── User Role Context
  │
  ▼
AI Routing Layer
  │
  ├── Finance AI Assistant
  ├── FP&A Copilot
  ├── Executive Insights Model
  └── Operations Intelligence Agent

Such an approach would allow organizations to provide tailored AI experiences while maintaining security, compliance, and governance requirements.

Although these capabilities are currently part of the long-term vision rather than the current implementation, they demonstrate how Context Engineering can evolve beyond dashboard state management and become a foundational layer for enterprise AI ecosystems.

More Than a Storage Widget

While ContextWallet currently provides capabilities such as context storage, retrieval, snapshot management, and AI payload generation, its long-term vision extends far beyond traditional state management.

At its core, ContextWallet represents an exploration of Context Engineering within SAP Analytics Cloud—a concept that I believe will become increasingly important as organizations integrate AI into their analytics ecosystems.

Today, business context is often fragmented across filters, input controls, application variables, and user selections. Human users can naturally understand this context because it is visible on the dashboard. AI systems, however, typically receive only the final question, without access to the business environment in which that question was asked.

ContextWallet attempts to bridge this gap by establishing a centralized context layer that can be shared across dashboards, applications, users, and AI services.

The objective is not simply to remember values.

The objective is to create a context-aware analytical environment where dashboards, business processes, and AI systems operate using a common understanding of the user's current business scenario.

Looking ahead, the same architecture could evolve beyond dashboard context management and become part of a broader AI governance and orchestration framework.

Future implementations could leverage context, session identifiers, role information, and security metadata to route requests to specialized AI models based on business function and user authorization.

For example:

  • Finance teams could interact with financial planning copilots.

  • FP&A users could access forecasting and variance analysis assistants.

  • Executives could receive strategic insight generation models.

  • Operations teams could utilize supply-chain-specific AI agents.

In such an architecture, ContextWallet would not only manage business context but also contribute to AI session management, security enforcement, model routing, and role-based AI experiences.

In other words, the future vision is not merely a smarter dashboard.

It is the creation of a shared context layer that enables analytics applications and AI systems to collaborate more effectively, securely, and intelligently.

The next section explores the technical architecture behind ContextWallet and demonstrates how it can act as an intermediary layer between SAP Analytics Cloud and AI platforms such as Databricks Genie.

Architecture and Technical Design

 

ContextWallet Architecture

 

 

After establishing the concept of Context Engineering, the next step was designing a practical implementation that could operate within SAP Analytics Cloud while remaining flexible enough to support future AI integrations.

The primary design objective was to create a lightweight, reusable context layer that could sit between dashboard interactions and AI systems.

Rather than allowing filters, selections, and application state to remain scattered throughout the application, ContextWallet centralizes this information into a structured context object that can be accessed by both SAC components and external services.

High-Level Architecture

At a high level, ContextWallet acts as an intermediary layer between user interactions and AI systems.

The workflow begins when users interact with dashboard components such as:

  • Input Controls

  • Dropdowns

  • Filters

  • Tables

  • Application Variables

  • Planning Selections

These interactions generate business context that is relevant to the current analytical session.

Instead of allowing each component to manage its own state independently, ContextWallet collects and stores this information in a centralized context repository.

The stored context can then be consumed by:

  • SAC Application Scripts

  • Dashboard Components

  • Snapshot Management Functions

  • AI Payload Builders

  • External AI Platforms such as Databricks Genie

This creates a single source of truth for business context throughout the application.

Context Lifecycle

The architecture follows a simple but effective lifecycle:

Step 1: Context Capture

User selections are captured from dashboard components and stored in ContextWallet.

For example:

ContextWallet_1.setContext({
    CompanyCode: "1000",
    FiscalYear: "2025",
    Version: "Actual",
    Currency: "USD"
});

At this stage, ContextWallet becomes aware of the user's analytical environment.

Step 2: Context Persistence

Once stored, the context remains available throughout the application lifecycle.

This enables:

  • Cross-page navigation

  • Multi-step workflows

  • Dashboard state retention

  • Consistent AI interactions

Instead of repeatedly requesting filter information from multiple controls, applications can retrieve the required context directly from ContextWallet.

Step 3: Context Enrichment

As users continue interacting with the dashboard, additional information can be added to the context.

For example:

ContextWallet_1.mergeContext({
    Entity: "EU_Corp",
    AccountType: "Revenue"
});

The context evolves alongside the user's analytical journey.

Step 4: Context Consumption

When an AI interaction occurs, ContextWallet combines the user's question with the active business context.

For example:

var payload =
ContextWallet_1.getAIPayload(
    "Why is revenue decreasing?"
);

Result:

{
  "question": "Why is revenue decreasing?",
  "context": {
    "CompanyCode": "1000",
    "FiscalYear": "2025",
    "Currency": "USD",
    "Entity": "EU_Corp",
    "AccountType": "Revenue"
  }
}

Rather than receiving an isolated question, the AI receives both the question and the business scenario in which it was asked.

Snapshot Engine

One of the core architectural components of ContextWallet is the Snapshot Engine.

Snapshots allow complete analytical states to be preserved and restored.

For example, a finance team conducting a month-end review may save the current context as:

ContextWallet_1.saveSnapshot(
    "MonthEndReview"
);

This snapshot captures the entire business context at that point in time.

Later, the same context can be restored using:

ContextWallet_1.loadSnapshot(
    "MonthEndReview"
);

This capability provides reproducibility for analytical workflows and establishes the foundation for future context comparison and AI memory scenarios.

Preparing for AI Integration

The architecture was intentionally designed with AI integrations in mind.

Traditional AI implementations typically receive only the user's prompt.

ContextWallet introduces an additional layer:

Question
+
Business Context
+
Session Context
+
Application State

This combination allows future AI systems to generate responses that are aware of:

  • Current dashboard filters

  • Active reporting periods

  • Business entities

  • User workflow state

  • Saved analytical scenarios

In essence, ContextWallet transforms dashboard interactions into structured knowledge that can be consumed by AI systems.

Extending the Architecture

Although the current implementation focuses on context management, the architecture has been designed to support future enhancements such as:

  • Context history tracking

  • Session management

  • AI routing

  • Role-based AI experiences

  • Context-aware security controls

  • Multi-agent orchestration

These capabilities could enable organizations to move beyond isolated AI assistants and toward fully context-aware analytics ecosystems.

The next section demonstrates how this architecture can be applied to a practical use case by integrating SAP Analytics Cloud with Databricks Genie through a shared context layer.

SAC + Databricks Genie: A Practical Context Engineering Use Case

The concept of Context Engineering becomes most valuable when we examine how SAP Analytics Cloud can interact with AI-powered analytics platforms such as Databricks Genie.

Databricks Genie enables users to ask business questions in natural language and receive insights directly from enterprise data. This significantly lowers the barrier to analytics by allowing business users to interact with data conversationally instead of relying solely on dashboards and reports.

 

 

 

However, when integrating SAC with Genie, a challenge immediately emerges.

The Context Gap

Consider a financial analyst working inside a SAC dashboard.

The analyst has already configured the dashboard using several business filters:

  • Company Code = 1000

  • Fiscal Year = 2025

  • Version = Actual

  • Currency = USD

  • Entity = EU_Corp

  • Account Type = Revenue

After reviewing the dashboard, the analyst asks:

Why is revenue lower than last year?

While the question appears complete from the user's perspective, it lacks the surrounding business context that exists within the dashboard.

Without access to the dashboard state, Genie may not know:

  • Which company is being analyzed

  • Which fiscal year is active

  • Which currency is being used

  • Which reporting version is selected

  • Which business entity is in focus

As a result, the AI system may generate a generic response or require additional clarification from the user.

This creates friction in the analytical workflow because users are forced to repeatedly provide information that already exists within the dashboard.

Context-Aware AI Interactions

This is where ContextWallet introduces a different approach.

Instead of sending only the user's question to Genie, ContextWallet can package both the question and the active dashboard context into a structured payload.

For example:

{
  "question": "Why is revenue lower than last year?",
  "context": {
    "CompanyCode": "1000",
    "FiscalYear": "2025",
    "Version": "Actual",
    "Currency": "USD",
    "Entity": "EU_Corp",
    "AccountType": "Revenue"
  }
}

From an AI perspective, this additional information is extremely valuable.

The question is no longer interpreted in isolation.

It is interpreted within the business scenario that the user is currently analyzing.

This allows AI systems to generate more focused, relevant, and actionable responses.

Improving User Experience

One of the most significant advantages of this approach is the reduction of repetitive user interactions.

Without Context Engineering:

User:
Why is revenue decreasing?

AI:
Which company?

User:
Company Code 1000.

AI:
Which fiscal year?

User:
2025.

AI:
Which reporting version?

User:
Actual.

With Context Engineering:

User:
Why is revenue decreasing?

AI:
Analyzes Company Code 1000,
Fiscal Year 2025,
Version Actual,
and provides a contextualized answer.

The user interacts naturally while the application provides the necessary context behind the scenes.

Beyond Questions and Answers

The potential value of ContextWallet extends beyond conversational analytics.

Future integrations could enable AI systems to access:

  • Current dashboard state

  • Saved snapshots

  • Historical analytical scenarios

  • User workflow context

  • Session metadata

  • Role information

This would allow AI systems to understand not only the current question but also the broader analytical journey of the user.

For example:

What changed since the Month-End Review?

Instead of treating this as a generic question, the AI could compare the current dashboard context with a previously saved snapshot and generate insights based on the differences.

Similarly, planning teams could compare forecast versions, executive teams could analyze strategic review snapshots, and finance teams could revisit previous reporting scenarios.

From Dashboards to Context-Aware Analytics

Traditional dashboards excel at presenting information.

AI systems excel at interpreting information.

However, without a shared context layer, these systems often operate independently.

ContextWallet attempts to bridge this gap by establishing a common context model that can be understood by both SAP Analytics Cloud and external AI platforms.

In this architecture, the dashboard becomes the source of business context, ContextWallet becomes the context management layer, and Databricks Genie becomes the intelligence layer.

Together, they create a foundation for a new generation of context-aware analytics applications where users can move seamlessly between dashboards, business workflows, and conversational AI experiences.

Future Vision: From Context Management to AI Orchestration

 

 

 

 

The current implementation of ContextWallet focuses on a relatively simple objective: capturing, preserving, and sharing business context within SAP Analytics Cloud.

However, while building and experimenting with the framework, it became apparent that the underlying concept could extend far beyond dashboard state management.

As organizations continue integrating AI into enterprise analytics, a new challenge emerges:

How do we manage, govern, and orchestrate multiple AI systems while maintaining security, context, and business relevance?

This is where the future vision of ContextWallet begins.

Beyond Dashboard Context

Today, ContextWallet primarily stores:

  • Dashboard filters

  • User selections

  • Application state

  • Analytical snapshots

  • AI payload context

In the future, the same architecture could be extended to manage additional layers of information such as:

  • Session Context

  • User Context

  • Security Context

  • Role Context

  • AI Routing Metadata

This would allow ContextWallet to evolve from a context repository into a centralized context orchestration layer.

AI Session Management

One of the most interesting future applications involves AI session management.

Enterprise AI interactions are rarely isolated events.

Users often engage in a sequence of related analytical conversations, building upon previous questions and insights.

For example:

Question 1:
Why is revenue decreasing?

Question 2:
Which products contributed most?

Question 3:
How does this compare with last quarter?

These questions are connected.

Without session awareness, each interaction must be treated independently.

With session-aware context management, AI systems can maintain continuity across analytical conversations and provide more relevant responses.

Future versions of ContextWallet could store session identifiers and maintain conversational context throughout the user's analytical journey.

Role-Based AI Experiences

Not every user requires access to the same AI capabilities.

A finance analyst, a controller, a planner, and an executive often have very different responsibilities and information requirements.

For example:

Finance Analyst

  • Variance Analysis

  • Financial Reporting

  • Cost Analysis

FP&A Team

  • Forecasting

  • Budget Planning

  • Scenario Simulation

Executive Leadership

  • Strategic Insights

  • KPI Summaries

  • Business Performance Reviews

Rather than exposing a single AI assistant to every user, future implementations could dynamically route requests to specialized AI systems based on role and authorization.

This would enable organizations to create tailored AI experiences while maintaining governance and security controls.

AI Routing and Model Selection

As AI ecosystems mature, organizations are unlikely to rely on a single model.

Instead, different AI systems may be optimized for different tasks.

For example:

Financial Analysis Model
        │
Forecasting Model
        │
Planning Copilot
        │
Executive Insights Assistant
        │
Operational Intelligence Agent

Future versions of ContextWallet could act as an intelligent routing layer.

Based on context, user role, and session metadata, requests could automatically be directed to the most appropriate AI system.

This would allow users to interact with a unified interface while the platform manages the complexity behind the scenes.

Security and Governance

Enterprise AI adoption introduces important governance requirements.

Organizations must ensure that users only access information relevant to their responsibilities.

ContextWallet could potentially support this through:

  • Role-aware context management

  • Secure session identifiers

  • Access-control metadata

  • AI authorization rules

For example, an executive assistant might receive organization-wide insights, while a finance analyst receives only information related to their assigned entities.

This approach would help ensure that AI interactions remain aligned with existing security and compliance frameworks.

Towards a Context-Aware Analytics Ecosystem

The long-term vision is not simply a smarter dashboard.

The vision is a context-aware analytics ecosystem where dashboards, applications, business processes, and AI systems operate using a shared understanding of the business environment.

In such an ecosystem:

  • Dashboards generate context.

  • ContextWallet manages context.

  • AI systems consume context.

  • Business users receive contextualized insights.

Instead of repeatedly explaining the business scenario to every application and AI assistant, context becomes a reusable enterprise asset.

Getting Started with ContextWallet

For readers interested in experimenting with ContextWallet, the project is available as an open-source SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget.

GitHub Repository

Source Code:

https://github.com/sampurno417/ContextWallet

The repository contains:

  • ContextWallet Widget Source Code

  • Widget Manifest (JSON)

  • Production Bundle

  • Documentation

  • Example Usage Patterns

Installation

Step 1: Download the Widget Manifest

Open the repository and download the latest ContextWallet JSON manifest.

Step 2: Import the Widget into SAP Analytics Cloud

Navigate to:

Analytics Designer
→ Custom Widgets
→ Add Custom Widget
→ Upload JSON Manifest

Once imported, ContextWallet becomes available within your SAC application.

Step 3: Add ContextWallet to the Application

Drag and drop the widget onto any page.

The widget itself does not need to be visible to end users and can be hidden after being added to the application.

ContextWallet is primarily designed as a backend utility component accessed through Analytics Designer scripting.

Basic Usage

Store Context

ContextWallet_1.setContext({
    CompanyCode: "1000",
    FiscalYear: "2025",
    Currency: "USD"
});

Retrieve Context

var context =
ContextWallet_1.getContext();

console.log(context);

Add Additional Fields

ContextWallet_1.addContextField(
    "Entity",
    "EU_Corp"
);

Merge Context

ContextWallet_1.mergeContext({
    AccountType: "Revenue"
});

Working with Snapshots

Save the current dashboard state:

ContextWallet_1.saveSnapshot(
    "MonthEndReview"
);

Retrieve available snapshots:

var snapshots =
ContextWallet_1.getSnapshots();

Restore a snapshot:

ContextWallet_1.loadSnapshot(
    "MonthEndReview"
);

Preparing AI Payloads

ContextWallet can generate structured payloads suitable for AI integrations.

var payload =
ContextWallet_1.getAIPayload(
    "Why is revenue decreasing?"
);

console.log(payload);

Example output:

{
  "question": "Why is revenue decreasing?",
  "context": {
    "CompanyCode": "1000",
    "FiscalYear": "2025",
    "Currency": "USD",
    "Entity": "EU_Corp"
  }
}

This payload can be passed to external AI systems such as Databricks Genie, custom LLM applications, or enterprise copilots.

Current Status

ContextWallet is currently an experimental framework focused on exploring Context Engineering concepts within SAP Analytics Cloud.

The project continues to evolve, with future enhancements planned around:

  • AI Session Management

  • Role-Based AI Routing

  • Security Context

  • Context History

  • Multi-Agent AI Integrations

  • Databricks Genie Connectivity

Feedback, suggestions, and collaboration ideas are always welcome.

Conclusion

As Generative AI becomes increasingly embedded within enterprise analytics platforms, context will play a critical role in determining the quality, relevance, and trustworthiness of AI-generated insights.

ContextWallet began as an experiment in solving a practical problem within SAP Analytics Cloud: preserving and sharing dashboard context.

Along the way, it revealed a broader architectural concept that I refer to as Context Engineering.

While the current implementation focuses on context management, snapshots, and AI payload generation, the underlying idea has the potential to evolve into a foundational layer for future AI-enabled analytics applications.

The journey is still at an early stage, but one thing is becoming increasingly clear:

In the era of enterprise AI, understanding the question is important.

Understanding the context behind the question is essential.

This project was developed as part of my exploration into SAP Analytics Cloud, AI-assisted analytics, and Databricks Genie integrations. I hope it encourages further discussion around Context Engineering and the role of context-aware architectures in the future of enterprise analytics.
